response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a versatile full-stack and distributed systems engineer with genuine strengths in high-throughput microservices and testing culture, but he is a poor fit for this role. The most critical disqualifier is the 8-year C++ requirement: his resume shows only ~6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a single C++ thesis project, with C++ absent entirely from his listed skills. His career is overwhelmingly oriented toward web technologies, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang rather than systems programming, cloud compute infrastructure, or hardware-adjacent platforms. He also lacks the required 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or compute/storage architecture experience, and has no demonstrated technical leadership in a cloud or DevOps context.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency design)
Strong testing and quality engineering culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored open-source D testing frameworks
Broad polyglot background with evidence of owning ambiguous, multi-year projects (GISCollective platform, Kubernetes deployments)
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI + a thesis project) against an 8-year minimum requirement — the single largest disqualifier
No cloud compute platform, storage, or hardware architecture experience; background is web/full-stack with limited infrastructure depth
No evidence of a formal technical leadership or mentoring role in cloud or DevOps engineering contexts
